selfImprovement.summary: Multi-layered autonomous improvement: (1) Overnight keyword rule probing and graduation at 1 AM, (2) needsPlan probe — 23 synthetic test cases evaluate 4B classifier accuracy, results in WhatsApp notification, (3) Trace analyser at 3 AM — analyses routing, plans, needsPlan F1, timing, anomalies from reasoning-traces.jsonl, (4) Ground truth harvester at 3:30 AM — extracts verifiable factual claims from yesterday's traces, searches authoritative sources, stores verified fact-source pairs, (5) Daily retrospective at 4 AM (bootstrap period) — 30B model reasons about top 3 priorities from trace data, auto-creates evolution tasks.
selfImprovement.evalSuite: 466+ tests across 94+ suites (task-planner, trace-analyser, reasoning-trace, weekly-retrospective, trigger, engagement, router, quality-gate, group-registry, output-filter, security-realworld, group-modes, pending-action, topic-scan, topic-index, etc). 88 tests for group analysis subsystem alone. 29 labeled needsPlan cases. Router eval covers keyword classification, write-intent detection, tool safety sets. Security tests cover 75 real-world attack vectors, 27 output filter scenarios, 21 group registry operations. Run with: npm test

theForge.summary: The Forge is my autonomous recursive self-improvement pipeline, replacing all previous overnight coding (overnight-coder.py, evo-evolve, self-improve cycle). It runs at 04:30, after all other overnight outputs are ready. Skills are the primary output — new capabilities as src/skills/ modules, not just bug fixes. Each skill has: name, canHandle(), execute(), selfExplanation. The skill registry auto-discovers skills at startup.
theForge.pipeline: 7-phase pipeline: (1) Intelligence Gathering — EVO 30B reads traces, logs, skills, past reports, ground truth, retrospective. (2) Architect — Opus via Claude Code CLI writes a detailed spec for the top opportunity. (3) Implement + Test — Opus implements with TDD on a forge/ git branch. (4) Review — fresh Opus session validates diff against spec, DGM evolutionary gate (must be improvement, not just correct). (5) Deploy or Queue — new skills auto-deploy (additive, sandboxed), existing file modifications queued for morning approval. (6) Meta-Improvement — targets eval/prompt improvements in data/forge/prompts/. (7) Report — generates overnight report, sends WhatsApp summary.
theForge.safeguards: Three-gate validation: architect classifies + tests pass + reviewer validates. Staged autonomy: new skills auto-deploy because they're additive and sandboxed as post-processing hooks. Existing file modifications need James's approval. forge-orchestrator.js itself is human-only — the Forge cannot modify its own orchestrator. Uses Opus via Max subscription (free on Max plan).
theForge.skillContract: Skills are post-processing hooks inserted after getClawdResponse() and before filterResponse(). They augment responses, never replace them. Contract: name, canHandle(msg, category), execute(msg, response), selfExplanation.
